name: Saddle Stitching
description: Hand-stitching method using two needles and waxed thread worked through pre-punched holes. Stronger than machine stitching because each stitch is independent.
steps: Mark stitch line with a wing divider set to 4 mm from the edge.
Punch holes along the stitch line with a 4-prong stitching chisel.
Cut a length of thread roughly 2.5× the stitch length.
Thread a needle on each end of the thread.
Push the first needle through hole 1; pull thread through to the midpoint.
Push the second needle through the same hole in the opposite direction, crossing the first thread.
Pull both ends tight and evenly, then repeat from step 5 for each hole.
Backstitch through the last 2 holes to lock, then trim and melt thread ends.
